name: Lil Hardin Armstrong
abstract: Lillian Hardin Armstrong (née Hardin; February 3, 1898 – August 27, 1971) was an American jazz pianist, composer, arranger, singer, and bandleader. She was the second wife of Louis Armstrong, with whom she collaborated on many recordings in the 1920s.

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Garland
about_quotes.evidence: So we organized our regular bunch. So that name, and I got a girl named Lil Harden. It's the name of Lil Armstrong... I put her in a place of band... So we had Joe Oliver, Nora Dutre... myself, and Lil.

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Garland
about_quotes.evidence: I got Lil to play with me, Lil Hardin, her name. Lil Armstrong... I asked her, could I use her for that night? ... So Lil said, yeah, I'll go play with you.

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Garland
about_quotes.evidence: I got Lil to play with me, Lil Hardin, her name... I had a job on the west side in Chicago, and I asked her, could I use her for that night?

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: I'd play at the Blue Note with Lil Armstrong... we were all in together. Played there for a long time.

about_quotes.speaker: Minor "Ram" Hall
about_quotes.evidence: So that wound up with Wilmer Breaux, Sugar Johnny, Lawrence Dewey, Roy Palmer, myself and Lil Hardin.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: I was there on the road with Lil Armstrong. We came in that winter to play the Apollo Theater.
